_note: PURLs below are pinned to the versions Trivy currently detects in this image: docker/docker@v28.5.2 (vendored by docker-buildx-plugin and docker-compose-plugin) and containerd/v2@v2.2.3 (real runtime dependency of the AL2023 `docker` dnf package, also vendored as library code by the same plugins). When `dnf install docker` resolves to a newer version, or the plugins bump their vendored libraries, this file must be reviewed: if the new versions are past the CVE fixes the entries can be removed; otherwise the PURLs must be updated.
author: runs-fleet maintainers

vulnerability.name: CVE-2026-34040
vulnerability.description: Docker authorization bypass via plugin disabling. Affects dockerd authorization handler.

statements.impact_statement: Present in docker-buildx-plugin and docker-compose-plugin as vendored library code only. Both binaries are Docker API clients; they do not run dockerd or its authorization handler. The vulnerable code path is dead code in the client binaries. The actual daemon (docker-ce) on this image is at 29.5.2 which contains the fix.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2026-41567
vulnerability.description: PUT /containers/{id}/archive executes container binary on the host. Daemon-side archive handler.

statements.impact_statement: Vendored in docker-buildx-plugin and docker-compose-plugin client binaries. Neither binary serves the /containers/{id}/archive endpoint; only dockerd does. Vulnerable handler code is unreachable from these binaries.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2026-42306
vulnerability.description: Race condition in docker cp allows bind mount redirection to host. Daemon-side cp handler.

statements.impact_statement: Vendored in docker-buildx-plugin and docker-compose-plugin client binaries. The race condition is in the daemon's cp implementation. Plugins talk to the daemon over the API; they do not execute the vulnerable cp handler themselves.

vulnerability.name: CVE-2026-46680
vulnerability.description: containerd user ID handling bypass allows runAsNonRoot evasion. Vulnerable code lives in containerd's CRI plugin, where a crafted image USER directive can bypass the Kubernetes-set runAsNonRoot SecurityContext check.

statements.impact_statement: Trivy reports this against dockerd (real containerd runtime dependency) and docker-buildx / docker-compose plugins (vendored as library code). The exploit requires a runAsNonRoot SecurityContext to flow through containerd's CRI plugin — a Kubernetes-only path. This AMI runs `docker run` via the legacy moby socket; CRI is not loaded and no Kubernetes pod spec is ever applied, so the user-ID resolution code path tied to runAsNonRoot enforcement is never exercised. The plugins are pure API clients that do not host the runtime at all.
